Abstract The inscription (K273) is written on the four faces of a stele and has 290 lines. Comprising 145 verses in Sanskrit, this inscription is one of the longest hitherto known to us. The contents deal with various topics. Among these manifold aspects of this inscription, a noticeable feature is the numerals, i.e. v. 51 gives the total quantity of rice consumed each year by the people related to this temple, then vv. 53-
